text: Want to walk into November 2 with nothing chasing me. Nothing. The brewhouse is enough.
Dave called. Says the back forty is dry. Told him I would worry when he worried. Note to self, reconcile after his inspection. Do not assume 64,000 just because that is what the contract says.
Reyes confident on the call. The Linear board does not agree with him quietly.
Reminder, do not let the spring release window slip. Someone is going to ask. The answer is no. The cellar timing is set against the bottle club and the May release.
Dave at the field. Light. He told me before I asked. He will send the report. He said he can find me the difference at spot, but the price is up.
Erin emailed the revised commitments. Some up, some down. Smoky Mountain pulling back on Lager. Erin wants Saison up sixty barrels. Reconcile carefully.
Reyes letter today. October 6 substantial completion. Four days. I will live with four days. I will not push the spring release window for four days.
Entered. Experimental Single-Origin Imperial Stout. Category 23, Barrel-Aged Strong Stout. Entry confirmed September 12. Entry # 4471-BBC-EXP01. Results announce October 8.
Not entered. Deadline passed. Greg asked. Answer was no, we are not pulling the experimental for it.

100% locally malted barley imperial stout. Eighteen months in barrel. First mash December 2027. NOT for the 2026-2027 season. Do not let anyone confuse this with the GABF entry. They are not the same beer.
Brasserie Dupont, Tourpes — saison brewing on a working farm, same family for generations
Brasserie de Blaugies — small, lambic-adjacent, kitchen-scale
